signals.idea: Make track labels part of the timeline canvas so they're always perfectly aligned with their rows. Currently track labels are in a separate scrolling column that can get out of sync with the canvas rows. The labels should be rendered inside the same scroll container as the timeline content, positioned at the left edge of each row, so they scroll together and stay aligned during drag-to-reorder. The drag handle should move both the label and the row content together as one unit.

unresolved_flags.concern: Track row dragging: Step 2 assumes the existing canvas rows can simply be wrapped in `useSortable`, but the real row stack is absolutely positioned rather than a normal list, so `verticalListSortingStrategy` will not behave like the current sidebar sortable list without a larger row-layout refactor or custom strategy.

Track row dragging: The execution order says inline labels can coexist with the old sidebar for validation, but `TrackLabel` already calls `useSortable({ id })`; rendering both versions at once would duplicate sortable registrations for the same track ids, and the plan never calls out extracting a non-sortable label component.

Timeline drop coordinates: Step 6 points the executor at `resolveDropTarget`, but that helper is not on the active drag/drop path; the repository computes both clip-drag and external-drop placement through `computeDropPosition`, so widening the inline label gutter without updating that path risks wrong drop times or row placement.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 6.0. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 3.

warnings: Scope creep detected: the plan appears to be expanding beyond the original idea or recorded user notes.

items: Track labels and clip rows are rendered inside the same scroll container — no separate scrolling column exists
Vertical scrolling keeps labels perfectly aligned with their rows at all times (no sync drift)
Horizontal scrolling keeps labels sticky at the left edge while clips scroll normally
Drag-to-reorder moves the entire row (label + clips) as a single visual unit
All existing clip interactions work: select, multi-select, drag between tracks, resize, split, delete, marquee select
External drag-drop from asset panel still places clips on correct tracks
Track label hover UI (rename, settings, delete) still functions
No regressions in coordinate calculations (clientXToTime, drop target resolution, clip positioning)

unresolved_flags.concern: Track row dragging: Step 2 assumes the existing canvas rows can simply be wrapped in `useSortable`, but the real row stack is absolutely positioned rather than a normal list, so `verticalListSortingStrategy` will not behave like the current sidebar sortable list without a larger row-layout refactor or custom strategy.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `TimelineCanvas` renders each row as `className="absolute left-0 right-0"` with `style={{ top, height: rowHeight }}` in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx:381-390`. The current sortable track list works in normal flow inside the sidebar column in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:263-292`.

unresolved_flags.concern: Track row dragging: The execution order says inline labels can coexist with the old sidebar for validation, but `TrackLabel` already calls `useSortable({ id })`; rendering both versions at once would duplicate sortable registrations for the same track ids, and the plan never calls out extracting a non-sortable label component.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `TrackLabel` owns its own sortable state in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TrackLabel.tsx:56-69`, and the existing sidebar already renders one `TrackLabel` per `track-${track.id}` in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:279-289`. Step 2 also proposes wrapping the whole row in `useSortable`, which would add another sortable layer unless `TrackLabel` is refactored first.

unresolved_flags.concern: Timeline drop coordinates: Step 6 points the executor at `resolveDropTarget`, but that helper is not on the active drag/drop path; the repository computes both clip-drag and external-drop placement through `computeDropPosition`, so widening the inline label gutter without updating that path risks wrong drop times or row placement.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `resolveDropTarget` is only defined in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/coordinate-utils.ts:119-133` and has no callers. The active path is `useDragCoordinator` calling `computeDropPosition` in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useDragCoordinator.ts:131-143`, which uses `startLeft` directly in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/drop-position.ts:73-99`; external asset/file drops reuse that coordinator in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:98-133`.
